channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Bryan Kohberger case: Idaho court releases surviving roommates' text messages from night of student murders
item.description: Text messages between two surviving roommates of four university students killed in a home invasion stabbing have been revealed for the first time.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Adnan Syed to stay free after judge decides on time served for his murder sentence in 'Serial' case
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Adnan Syed, whose case amassed a worldwide following of “Serial” podcast listeners, will remain free — even though his murder conviction still stands, a Baltimore judge ruled on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE and BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: A South Carolina man is set to become the first executed by firing squad in 15 years
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — When the clock strikes 6 Friday evening, a South Carolina man will walk into the death chamber, be strapped into a chair and have a target placed over his heart.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump says he will 'probably' extend TikTok ban deadline if deal isn't reached in time
item.description: President Trump on Thursday suggested he would consider extending the deadline for a ban on TikTok for a second time if a deal isn't reached, stating there is "a lot of interest" in the video-sharing platform.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, KTLA 5

item.title: Life-saving avalanche forecasts could be hindered by Trump budget cuts
item.description: LEADVILLE, Colo. (AP) — Officials in the Western U.S. who warn the public about avalanches are sounding a different type of alarm.
item.dc:creator: BRITTANY PETERSON and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Medical plane’s voice recorder likely wasn’t working for years before Philadelphia crash
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board says a cockpit voice recorder was not working on a medical transport plane that crashed in Philadelphia in January and likely had not been working for several years.
item.dc:creator: Maryclaire Dale, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: CDC rehiring some laid-off workers
item.description: Administration officials told the CDC last month that nearly 1,300 of its probationary employees would be cut, but only approximately 700 to 750 employees ended up receiving termination notices.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: First national analysis finds America's butterflies are disappearing at 'catastrophic' rate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — America's butterflies are disappearing because of insecticides, climate change and habitat loss, with the number of the winged beauties down 22% since 2000, a new study finds.
item.dc:creator: SETH BORENSTEIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: California's Gavin Newsom opposes trans athletes in women's sports, splitting with progressives
item.description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom has spoken out against allowing transgender women and girls to compete in female sports.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Police identify 2 people who died in Feb. 19 midair collision at southern Arizona airport
item.description: MARANA, Ariz. (AP) — Authorities have publicly identified two people killed two weeks ago in the midair collision of two small planes at a southern Arizona airport. Police say 70-year-old Michael Reinath and 76-year-old Linda Gifford died in the Feb.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Derek Chauvin pardon 'would set us back as a society': Minnesota AG
item.description: Minnesota’s attorney general is pushing back on calls for President Donald Trump to pardon Derek Chauvin, the white police officer convicted of murdering George Floyd in 2020.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, Fox 5

item.title: Roy Ayers,'Everybody Loves the Sunshine' musician, passes away at 84
item.description: Los Angeles native, Roy Ayers, who was behind the popular song "Everybody Loves the Sunshine" has passed away. He was 84 years old.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WGN 9

item.title: Chipotle expands menus with new protein option
item.description: Chipotle customers can now customize their tacos, burritos, salads and quesadillas with a new protein option. The chain announced Thursday that it's adding Chipotle Honey Chicken to menus in the United States, Canada and the United Kingdom on March 7.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Things to know about avalanches, what causes them and how to stay safe
item.description: ANCHORAGE, Alaska (AP) — Three skiers are believed dead after an avalanche swept them away and buried them under a pile of snow nearly 10 stories deep in the Alaska backcountry. Each winter about 25 people on average die in avalanches in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: The controversy behind California's swastika-shaped building complex
item.description: The Navy even went public on its website with a statement regarding the buildings, saying it understands their configuration is a "sensitive topic.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, NewsNation

item.title: Vance suggests other industries won't get tariff carve-outs
item.description: Vice President Vance on Wednesday said that industries other than U.S. automakers have asked for exemptions on tariffs, but suggested that the Trump administration would not extend further carve outs.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: Trump grants one-month exemption for US automakers from new tariffs on imports from Mexico, Canada
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump is granting a one-month exemption on his stiff new tariffs on imports from Mexico and Canada for U.S. automakers, as worries persist that the newly launched trade war could crush domestic manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK and ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Two men charged after Kansas City Chiefs fans found in backyard
item.description: Two men have been charged in connection to the deaths of three Kansas City Chiefs fans who were found dead in the backyard of a home in January 2024.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, Fox 5

item.title: Baja Midnight, Quesocrisp Taco, and more: What’s coming to Taco Bell in 2025
item.description: Taco Bell announced a slate of new menu items and plans for the fast food chain at its second annual Live Mas event, which took place in New York City on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, WFLA, NBC

item.title: ICE releases criminal histories of illegal immigrant mother and son arrested in California amid backlash
item.description: ICE is defending itself after it arrested a mother and son pair of illegal immigrants with criminal histories in the United States, the agency said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Two men charged after Kansas City Chiefs fans found in backyard
item.description: Two men have been charged in connection to the deaths of three Kansas City Chiefs fans who were found dead in the backyard of a home in January 2024.
item.dc:creator: Olivia Johnson, ABC 4

item.title: Griffith Observatory to stream total lunar eclipse live online
item.description: Griffith Observatory will host a live online broadcast of the total lunar eclipse on March 13-14, allowing viewers to watch the celestial event remotely. The broadcast will begin at 8:50 p.m. on the Observatory’s YouTube channel and run through 3:05 a.m.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, KTLA 5

item.title: Man treated 'like a dog' before murder in upstate New York, prosecutor says
item.description: CANANDAIGUA, N.Y. (AP) — Seven people accused of killing a 24-year-old man in upstate New York subjected him to weeks of torture that included sexual assault, forcing him to eat feces and pouring bleach on him, prosecutors said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Marines and soldiers discharged for refusing the COVID-19 vaccine show limited interest in rejoining
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Roughly 650 Marines and Army soldiers who were forced out of the military for refusing to get the COVID-19 vaccine have so far expressed interest in returning to the force under an executive order from President Donald Trump, officials said this week.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Three Michigan brothers missing since 2010 are declared dead by a judge at mom's request
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — A judge declared three Michigan brothers dead Wednesday, more than 14 years after they disappeared at Thanksgiving in a small-town tragedy that remains unsolved despite an explicit belief by investigators that their father is responsible.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Mötley Crüe postpones Las Vegas residency due to vocalist Vince Neil's health
item.description: Mötley Crüe announced they are postponing their upcoming Las Vegas residency due to vocalist Vince Neil's health.
item.dc:creator: Linsey Lewis, Fox 5

item.title: Trump admin plans to cut 80,000 employees from Veterans Affairs: internal memo
item.description: The Department of Veterans Affairs is planning an “aggressive” reorganization that includes cutting 80,000 jobs from the sprawling agency that provides health care for retired military members, according to an internal memo obtained by The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Michigan brothers missing since 2010 declared dead at mom's request
item.description: A judge declared three Michigan brothers dead Wednesday, more than 14 years after they disappeared at Thanksgiving in a small-town tragedy that remains unsolved.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 31 million Americans borrowed money for health care last year: Poll
item.description: (The Hill) — More than 31 million Americans borrowed money last year to pay for health care, a new survey found. Those Americans borrowed about $74 billion, despite most of them have some form of health insurance, the West Health-Gallup survey found.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, Fox 5

item.title: Child with cancer given Secret Service badge during Trump speech
item.description: More than a dozen Democrats joined Republicans in a standing ovation for a guest of Trump, a child diagnosed with cancer who Trump said aspires to become a police officer. The child, named DJ, was held up by his father as Republicans and attendees in the House gallery chanted “DJ.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Watch: Fishermen rush to save 900-pound dolphin that landed on their boat
item.description: A placid fishing trip turned “berserk” for three New Zealand men when a dolphin weighing more than 900 pounds appeared to fall out of the sky before crash-landing in their small boat.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Boy reportedly kidnapped from Atlanta 7 years ago found in Colorado; 2 arrested
item.description: A 14-year-old boy who went missing seven years ago has been located, some 1,400 miles from where he was allegedly abducted.
item.dc:creator: Morgan Whitley, WGN 9

item.title: Supreme Court denies Trump administration request to cancel $2 billion in foreign aid
item.description: The Supreme Court in a 5-4 emergency ruling Wednesday refused to halt a judge’s decision ordering the Trump administration to immediately release nearly $2 billion in foreign aid payments owed under existing contracts.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9

item.title: More states want to stop taxing groceries
item.description: The number of states imposing sales taxes on groceries has shrunk over the years, and the number may decrease further in the coming months
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Elissa Slotkin assails Trump's early actions, offers Democrats a way to fight back
item.description: LANSING, Mich. (AP) — First-term Michigan Sen. Elissa Slotkin accused President Donald Trump of driving up costs while pushing for an “unprecedented giveaway to his billionaire friends” in Tuesday night's Democratic response to his first joint congressional address of his second term.
item.dc:creator: JOEY CAPPELLETTI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Emboldened Trump praises his 'swift action' in address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump will stand before a joint session of Congress on Tuesday to give an accounting of his turbulent first weeks in office as a divided nation struggles to keep pace, with some Americans fearing for the country's future while others are cheering him on.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Is there a secret behind the Netflix intro sound? Some viewers seem to think so
item.description: The origin of the familiar sound has become a topic of conversation among online conspiracy theorists in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, ABC 4
